description: Evaluates the DSVSR meta-cognitive reasoning engine across different complexity levels, confidence thresholds, and edge cases. Each eval targets a specific protocol stage or decision point.

evals.name: full-dsvsr-complex-problem
evals.prompt: Should we migrate our monolithic Python Django app to microservices using Go, or refactor the monolith with better module boundaries? We have 15 developers, 2M monthly active users, and deploy twice a week.
evals.expected_output: Full DSVSR protocol: decomposes into 3-7 sub-problems, solves each with confidence scores, verifies across 4 dimensions, synthesizes with global confidence, and reflects on weaknesses.
expectations: DECOMPOSE produces 3-7 sub-problems with dependency mapping
Each sub-problem has explicit confidence score with justification
VERIFY checks logic, facts, completeness, and bias for each sub-answer
Verification changes at least one confidence score (or documents why all confirmed)
SYNTHESIZE computes global confidence using weighted formula
Metadata block includes global confidence, sub-problem scores, sources, weaknesses, rigidity level
Conflict between options explicitly resolved with trade-off analysis

evals.name: fast-path-simple-question
evals.prompt: What is the difference between a LEFT JOIN and an INNER JOIN in SQL?
evals.expected_output: Fast path activated: answers directly without full DSVSR. Fewer than 2 complexity signals detected.
expectations: Does NOT run full DSVSR protocol (single-domain, factual, low ambiguity)
Answers directly with clarity and relevant caveats
No sub-problem decomposition (unnecessary overhead for simple question)
Implicit high confidence (factual, verifiable answer)
Response is concise and direct, not wrapped in DSVSR framework

evals.name: low-confidence-escalation
evals.prompt: Will quantum computing make current encryption obsolete within 5 years, and should we start preparing our banking infrastructure now?
evals.expected_output: DSVSR with multiple sub-problems below 0.70 confidence. Flags speculative sub-problems, escalates uncertainty, and presents options rather than a single recommendation.
expectations: At least one sub-problem flagged below 0.70 confidence (quantum timeline is speculative)
Never inflates confidence on inherently uncertain predictions
REFLECT identifies weakest sub-problem and diagnoses gap type (data gap, unknowable)
Fundamentally unknowable aspects stated clearly (exact quantum timeline)
Rigidity level set to 'exploratory' (multiple valid paths)
Presents options rather than a single recommendation given low confidence

evals.name: circular-dependency-edge-case
evals.prompt: Design a pricing strategy for our SaaS product where the price depends on the number of users, but the number of users depends on the price. Also factor in competitor pricing and our cost structure.
evals.expected_output: DECOMPOSE detects circular dependency between price and user count. Merges them into a single sub-problem or introduces an assumption to break the cycle.
expectations: Detects circular dependency between pricing and user count sub-problems
Resolves cycle by merging into single sub-problem or introducing breaking assumption
Breaking assumption is explicitly stated and flagged as a key sensitivity
Other sub-problems (competitor analysis, cost structure) handled independently
Global confidence accounts for the introduced assumption
Metadata documents the circular dependency resolution approach

evals.name: custom-confidence-target
evals.prompt: Analyze whether our Node.js API can handle 10x traffic growth. Target confidence: 0.90. We currently serve 500 RPS with p99 latency of 200ms.
evals.expected_output: DSVSR with custom 0.90 confidence target. REFLECT loop continues until 0.90 reached or identifies what would be needed to reach it.
expectations: Custom confidence target of 0.90 honored (not default 0.95)
Sub-problems include capacity analysis, bottleneck identification, scaling strategy
Concrete data points used: 500 RPS baseline, 5000 RPS target, 200ms p99 current
If confidence < 0.90, REFLECT identifies what additional info would close the gap
Maximum 3 REFLECT retries enforced to prevent infinite loops
Would-increase-to field populated for sub-problems below target
